mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals economic deceleration and reduced corporate earnings expectations, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 60 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, leading to negative quarterly equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns dominate when both RSAFS and HOUST decline concurrently over three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2 percent annualized signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and strong corporate output, driving positive equity returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of periods when INDPRO annualized growth exceeds 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment indicate a restrictive monetary stance causing labor market stress and credit tightening, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0 percent while UNRATE increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1 percent annualized signals severe aggregate demand weakness, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the following week as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow GDP growth readings below 1 percent annualized in more than half of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenue growth, generating positive quarterly equity returns through improved earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns dominate during periods of consistent PAYEMS growth exceeding 100 thousand per month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stable or falling policy rates erodes real yields and signals delayed central bank response, causing negative forward equity returns due to valuation compression.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rising AND FEDFUNDS flat or falling
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur when CPIAUCSL increases while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged or declines.
